raw_response:
**Overall rating: FALSE**

GitHub’s own docs say `Co-authored-by` commits are visible on GitHub and can count in the profile contributions graph, so the claim that the co-author is **not** added to the contributors graph is incorrect. The stronger evidence also suggests GitHub recognizes co-authors in commit attribution rather than limiting credit to only the primary author.[1]

## Claim-by-claim analysis

| Claim | Rating | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| “Co-Authored-By trailers in git commits are displayed by github.com as small avatars on the PR/commit page.” | TRUE | GitHub documents that `Co-authored-by` trailers make a commit visible on GitHub, and community reports describe co-authors being shown as avatars/attribution in the UI. GitHub’s docs confirm multi-author commit attribution, which supports this UI behavior. [1][2][3] |
| “the named co-author is NOT added to the repository's main contributors graph at /graphs/contributors.” | FALSE | GitHub explicitly states that co-authored commits “can be included in the profile contributions graph and the repository’s statistics,” and the contributors graph docs say contributors are based on commit contributions, not just the primary author field. [1][1] |
| “The contributors graph only counts the primary author of each commit.” | FALSE | GitHub’s documentation contradicts this: co-authored commits can count, and the contributors graph includes commit contributors that meet GitHub’s criteria. [1][1] |

## Summary

The content is mostly wrong because it misstates how GitHub handles co-authored commits. GitHub documents that co-authored commits can appear in contribution graphs and repository statistics, not just as avatar attribution on commit/PR pages.[1]

## Sources

- GitHub Docs: [Creating a commit with multiple authors](https://docs.github.com/en/enterprise-server@3.17/pull-requests/committing-changes-to-your-project/creating-and-editing-commits)[1]
- GitHub Docs: [Viewing a project's contributors](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/viewing-activity-and-data-for-your-repository/viewing-a-projects-contributors)[4]
- GitHub Docs: [Creating a commit with multiple authors](https://docs.github.com/articles/creating-a-commit-with-multiple-authors)[5]
